transcript.text: Good, thanks.
Hey, Mara.
Hey, how's it going?
Hey, Luke, how are you?
Good.
are you?
Doing well.
Vivek, how's the weather in Portugal?
It's probably somewhere, it's like 38 Celsius today, so yeah.
That's great.
toasty, yes.
That's awesome.
In New York, it's like this consistent overcast of ominous rain that hasn't quite broken yet, but whatever, it'll be swamp-like in a few weeks, so I actually shouldn't complain.
Yeah.
How's the weather for you?
Me?
Yeah.
That's great.
I'm in Maine.
Oh, amazing.
Which part of Maine?
It's called Bailey Allen's in Casco Bay.
Cool.
Okay.
That's awesome.
I hope you eat a lot of lobster rolls.
Thank you.
Hey, everyone.
Hello.
How's it going?
Well, how are you?
It's well.
Are we waiting for anyone else or it looks like we kind of have...
I think we have everyone, yeah.
Can you just share my screen?
So, yeah, just diving into the performance reports for the block pages.
And just like a preface to this, I sort of readjusted the grid, the grid lines in the background so that they weren't looking as messy.
So you might see this red sort of the sessions line like a bit deeper in the graph now just because of the scales being adjusted a bit.
But yeah, so the...
the...
Additional data on what's going on over here, unfortunately, it's just, it seems to be dropping.
The only thing we can think of is perhaps LLM overviews, but even then, the drop seems pretty drastic for that.
Generally, compared to last week, SEMrush is now showing even more URLs showing up in AI overviews.
Currently, 185 of these pages are showing up across about 2,600 keywords.
We seem to be showing up from a bunch of different types of keywords, not all of them connected to expense categories.

Helps with the visibility, for sure, but we definitely have lost a lot of clicks over here, and it seems to be sort of heading downward for now, but we just have to see whether they stabilize at some point.
And until we have more data, unfortunately, we can't really dig into this more.
So that's kind of where we're at right now.
now.
For the tuning pages, we saw a bit of a dip in GA4 data for this week, but then GSC data showed us something else with this weird hump over here for the impressions.
Again, not sure why, like what is going on here.
GSC has been pretty volatile on the date.
So this drop in clicks isn't as drastic as it seems here in GA4.
So generally, we think waiting another week just to see if this plays out or if it averages out, as is usually the case between GSC and GA4, just see if that smooths out over another week.
If this, of course, continues, then we'll dig deeper and see whether there's anything that has changed in regards to competitor sort of positions in the SERP.
There weren't any significant drops, though, for any single page.
It just seemed like a lot of the pages, all the pages in time, just lost one session here and there, which kind of dragged performance down.
So, yeah.
As I mentioned, we're kind of thinking, like, given what the GSC is showing us, it seems like perhaps waiting for another week is the best move over here.

For the second batch of tuning pages, not super exciting performance, unfortunately, but generally, as we can see from this chart, post-refresh, we are getting more sessions and views and also clicks on these pages compared to pre-refresh.
So generally good news, obviously, we'd like to see much more, but given where these pages were, we certainly are in a much better position.
The treasury pages saw some higher performance this week, generally higher sessions and views.
The liquidity position report page was the top performer, which is interesting because it doesn't seem to have any direct sort of keyword searches in Google, at least not high-volume ones.
So it's kind of interesting that that suddenly...
Showed up, but generally the pages are, they seem to be in this kind of a range, like maxing out at close to 30 sessions, sorry, 30 views and about 10 to 15 sessions or thereabout.
Regarding the vendor pages, we're seeing again performance sort of hover around this particular level, close to about 25, 26 sessions and close to 100 views.
So we seem to be in this particular range generally, though, as we can see past four weeks, we seem to be ending upwards, so that's quite encouraging.
But yeah, that's generally the overview of all the different page categories.
Any questions I can answer about any of this, any clarifications?
Any questions?
All right.
Just wanted to check.
I know Matt had mentioned this last week, but the zero traffic pages, refreshing them, do you want us to go ahead and get started with that?
Or is there any internal techs you'd like to run before getting started?
I just want to get your thoughts on that.
Yeah, totally.
So I am going to be taking over that moment going forward.
I think everybody's on the call, so we probably just chatted out.
It shouldn't take that long.

Basically, what's holding me up is once we have the P1 targets nailed down, do we want to take another look at these pages for overlap or cannibalization concerns before going through with the rewrites?
Or do we just say, whatever, just push it, and then we can take them down later once we have time to actually do a deeper dive?
Which is...
Anyone have strong feelings on that?
Because otherwise, I think we can just get started right away.
Yeah, I think we can start with the ones that have low traffic now.
Okay.
And if you did, we'll...
Cool.
Yeah, that works for me.

Yeah, I think, like, if y'all have any opinions on it, Matt, like, sorry if you've already, like, narrowed, or, like, finalized the details, I think, yeah, we just want to, like, have really good visibility into, like, what's in the queue, and, like, what's been done.
Just so, yeah, like, if there is ever the case of, like, oh, we tuned something, and it does really well, and then it, you know, takes from something we're, like, super focused on.
But whether that's, like, Airtable again, or, yeah, like, I think...
I would...
Like not on the call, just because I think like others besides Matt won't probably won't join unless there's like a specific project they're working with you on.
Yeah, maybe it's like, I don't know.
How do you do this with other people now?
Just Airtable?
Pretty much in Airtable.
So we can follow the same process.
Like we can create a separate tab for these articles and then we can put the list in there with a go no go.
So if you feel like there might be some criminalization concerns, you can maybe just flag that to us and we just won't, we'll skip over it.
So that's the process we've been following for the vendor pages as well.
And I think it's been working pretty smoothly.
So yeah, all you have to do is just focus on that tab and we'll take care of the rest in terms of moving the statuses to the main tab and all that stuff.
Okay.
Matt, like given how we want the system to work, is it okay to have the output in Airtable?
That's weird.
We have to hack something else together to do that.
But output, you mean like the actual content of the draft?
The targets for them to work on.
Oh, I see.
No, I think that should be fine.
Like, the way that I have the workflow set up for the P2 stuff right now, it shouldn't ever surface anything that would wind up on the rewrite list.
So I'm not concerned about overlap there.
In terms of visibility into what we're actually touching, like, I still need to wrap my head around how we're going to do that.
I think the growthx air table obviously is the place to start that we can act as kind of like the source of truth.
Then I also was thinking we should add growthx as a publishing team in Sanity so that we can start looking at those posts specifically in Looker, if that makes sense to you.
So we can tag.
As SEO and growthx, or whatever.
That should work.
Okay.
So then, I mean, if we have a report set up that just pulls for those posts, we'll see everything that's never touched, basically.
Yeah.
All right.
Okay, cool.
So, yeah, I mean, it doesn't sound like there's anything more to be done on our side in terms of, like, prepping those URLs for you guys to take a look at.

But given this is my first time actually working with you all on, like, the content outputs, I think I'd like to just take a quick look at maybe the first, like, five or ten to see if there's anything we can add back into the brand guidelines that you all are using to try to just, like, get the output really tight so that we can limit the review on those.
Yeah, for sure.
All right.
I can send over the list of URLs, put it in Airtable, send it over your review, and, yeah, just tag you as a...
We have those first batch of articles ready.
Okay, sweet.
Sounds good.
All right.
And I just wanted to check in regarding the vendor pages, the best bank pages.
I just wanted to check if you've had a chance to look at those.
Anything you can change over there, etc.
Nothing is needed on the best banks articles on your site anymore.
Thank you.
Okay.
Yeah, just going to edit those.
Okay, great.
And for the vendor pages, I changed the status for a few of them to be added to the CMS.
So for the previous one, you guys added the alternatives and then the versus.
I'm hoping that for this upcoming one, we can add the main page as well.
So I recorded another Loom and added added to that.
Same doc with details on adding that main page.
So I'll get the data wrapper ID to you, and yeah, we should be good to move forward.
Okay, good stuff.
We will get on that by tomorrow, and then we'll finish that off.
yeah.
Awesome.
Thank you.
Victoria, let's say our test rounds out really well.
Well, if we were to make 20 times the number of these from growthx side, is there anything that's preventing that from happening?
Or I'm sure there's many things, but it's not super time sensitive, but I think this test is probably going to round out positively, and we would like to very much expand the footprint for vendors.
But yeah, if we do it, it should be like very large.
So...
If there's anything you think that's very slow that would cause us to need two months to put up 400 URLs instead of a couple weeks, we would definitely want to hear about it.
And then we're going to do the same on our end.
One thing we talked about was the way that the pages are structured in Sanity.
There are some sections that are reusable, like we add them to the main page, and then we don't need to add them again, the child pages.
And right now, for the workflow that we're using, it creates these sections over and over, just because it's the initial, it's the first one.
And we decided not to change until we were, we were finished with this initial.

And these, these few batches that were assigned just so we wouldn't delay, maybe we can, we can think about putting together a new workflow that will save time on the content review, because right now we are still outputting a lot more content that will, that will actually be needed for the pages.
Any thoughts, Vivek?
None come to mind right now, but I will circle back with you, just after, just need to sort of review this and get back to you, if that's okay.
Yeah, that sounds good.
And then other than that, I think we have a good workflow in place in terms of reviewing and publishing.
So, So, So,
Yeah, I don't see other bottlenecks that would prevent us from scaling.
Okay, so it sounds like we should create a new component, ideally programmatically, and then that component's used in the other, like the child pages, instead of just redoing the content.

I mean, that's already happening in the way that the pages are structured in Sanity, so a lot of the sections for the child pages are actually being picked up from the main page, but in terms of how we are producing the content, we're still producing everything for each page, does that make sense?
So we're producing the sections individually, per page.
So is that just like increasing like review overhead?
Yes.
Oh, okay.
I mean, it's not...
Yeah.

What we're doing, like, we're just keeping, from my end, I'm just keeping the sections, but I know that there is the, like, you guys also reviewed the content internally, and I assume that that also takes back, but it's also not a huge bottleneck.
I mean, we can, we, the maximum we were able to get per week of the, the number of pages was around 22, right, Vivek?
Correct me if I'm wrong.
Yeah.
Yes, we did about 22 to 23, that, two, two, three weeks back.
Fair enough.
So, yeah, the goal would be...
Increase the number of pages per week, so, yeah, that would be an attempt to, like, changing the workflow to have less content to review would increase the number of pages per week.
That's my thought.
Well, what is the, do you have a specific target number of pages in mind?
know Luke just mentioned, like, up 400, but.
Yeah, it could be, like, infinite.
We'll give you a better target, and that's obviously more helpful, but.
Yeah, because we can certainly take it to our engineering team and see if we can build, like, some sort of a custom workflow.
So, um, I don't know if, how, I don't know if it's possible to API into Sanity and even maybe directly publish, because we're doing that on Webflow quite a bit.
So, so yeah, I don't, I don't think we'll get to this.

Point for the next version, but our content team uses our, and we talked about like giving you guys access previously to like our spend data and merchant data to do this, but their output's good and I don't think we're there yet.

But so basically anytime they ever speak about a vendor, like write about them, any kind of spend report, we basically would want to make sure we have the vendor page up within like a business day or something.
And then similarly for like really high priority software companies that our outbound team is targeting, which like that's in the thousands, like very, very quickly.
So whether it's like, yeah, we batch them all, like manually pulling these lists, which just sounds like a lot of overhead or, or there's just like.

50 to 100 per week, like I think that's probably three, but I think like for this next patch, we will just want to like see what falls apart if we try to do a couple hundred at once or within a shorter timeframe.
Okay.
Okay.
We'll take a look internally as well, see if we can tweak those flows and maybe generate more.
Because as Victoria said, even we're kind of following a process where we have to generate these one by one.
Maybe if there's a way to generate some of these in bulk with one, a few clicks that might help you guys much more.
So we'll take a look at that.
Okay.
Cool.
All right.
I think that was pretty much it.
One quick question.
So what exactly is it from like from our requirements for these pages that makes us have to produce?
One by one versus invoke, what's the, what would it be losing?
It's kind of, it's actually a bit of a limitation of Aerox right now.
Like we only customize it to a certain extent.
So the reason it goes one by one is because we have to generate one article per line, per row in the grid.
We cannot build a custom workflow on Aerox that says that, okay, for these batch of pages with these particular elements, just create them all at once with say a few clicks.
We can't build that yet.
We are moving to a custom platform in a few weeks time away from Aerox.
So that, that's why I mentioned that we could take this to the engineering team and see if we can build something custom for you that allows us to scale.
So that's where I was coming from.
Does that answer your question or?
It does.
So even if we were to.
Leave the pages more structured, like less personalized, we still can't produce them in bulk right now.
We can, well, it depends on what bulk is.
We can certainly increase from, say, 22 a week to maybe, I would say, 30 a week, about, which something tells me that doesn't match your definition of bulk, given the numbers we were discussing.
But that's because, again, it's just like, we have to go row by row and just click one by one and sort of do it that way.
Any more than that, like, truly at scale, it kind of needs like a custom workflow, which we can certainly build out, and we'll be in a better position to do that in a few weeks' time.
That's good.
That should be good, Dan.
Yeah, the only...

Solution to go from, say, 30 to, like, 100 is to literally throw more bodies at the problem, like, you know, I don't know, clone Nika like 10 times over and have people running those things and, you know, that sort of thing.
So it's just a limitation of aerobs where we are at right now.
Okay.
All right.
Thanks, everyone.
So I will follow up with, uh, everything else on, uh, on Slack and get back to you.
Thank you.
Thank you.
you.
Thanks.
Nice to you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly check-in between Ramp and GrowthX teams to discuss performance reports, content strategies, and scaling vendor pages.](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Block pages showing declining performance; potentially due to LLM overviews](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=93.0)
  - [Tuning pages experiencing volatility; team to monitor for another week](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=240.0)
  - [Zero traffic pages refresh to begin; Matt taking over project management](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [Vendor pages scaling discussed; aiming for significant expansion (potentially 400+ URLs)](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=809.0)
  - [Current workflow limitations identified; GrowthX moving to custom platform in weeks to enable scaling](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=1296.0)

## Topics

### Performance Reports Analysis

  - [Block pages: Declining performance (185 pages across 2,600 keywords)](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=193.0)
      - [Drastic drop in clicks, potentially due to LLM overviews](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=186.0)
      - [More data needed for deeper analysis](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=231.0)
  - [Tuning pages:](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=240.0)
      - [GA4 shows dip, GSC data volatile with impression spike](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=240.0)
      - [No significant drops for individual pages](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=287.0)
      - [Team to monitor for another week before further action](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [Second batch tuning pages:](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=312.0)
      - [Post-refresh showing improvement (more sessions/views/clicks)](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=312.0)
      - [Performance not "super exciting" but better than pre-refresh](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=328.0)
  - [Treasury pages:](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=338.0)
      - [Higher performance this week (sessions and views up)](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=338.0)
      - [Liquidity position report page top performer (\~30 views, 10-15 sessions)](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=345.0)
  - [Vendor pages:](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=372.0)
      - [Performance hovering around 25-26 sessions, \~100 views](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=372.0)
      - [Upward trend over past four weeks](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=385.0)

### Content Strategy Updates

  - [Zero traffic pages refresh:](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
      - [Matt taking over project management](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=439.0)
      - [Team to start with low-traffic pages](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=486.0)
      - [Process: Create separate Airtable tab, use go/no-go system for potential cannibalization](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=558.0)
  - [Best bank pages: No further edits needed on Ramp's site](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=739.0)
  - [Vendor pages:](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=372.0)
      - [Victoria updated statuses for CMS addition](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=750.0)
      - [Main page to be added in upcoming batch](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=776.0)
      - [Data wrapper ID to be provided](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=785.0)

### Scaling Vendor Pages

  - [Goal: Significantly expand vendor page footprint (potentially 400+ URLs)](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=809.0)
  - [Current max output: \~22-23 pages/week](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=1068.0)
  - [Limitations:](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=1266.0)
      - [Content review overhead due to repetitive section generation](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=1274.0)
      - [Aerox platform constraints (one-by-one page creation)](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=1282.0)
  - [Potential solutions:](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=1296.0)
      - [GrowthX moving to custom platform in weeks](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=1296.0)
      - [Possibility of building custom workflow for scaling](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=1303.0)
      - [Exploring bulk generation options to reduce manual work](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=1330.0)

### Workflow Optimization

  - [Sanity CMS structure allows section reuse, but content still produced individually](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=1006.0)
  - [Team considering new workflow to reduce content review time](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=1100.0)
  - [Exploring possibilities of API integration with Sanity for direct publishing](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=1127.0)
  - [GrowthX to internally review processes for potential bulk generation methods](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=1360.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Vivek to send list of zero traffic page URLs to Matt for review](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=711.0)
  - [Victoria to provide data wrapper ID for vendor pages](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=785.0)
  - [GrowthX team to begin vendor page additions to CMS by tomorrow](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=796.0)
  - [Luke to provide more specific target number for vendor page scaling](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=809.0)
  - [GrowthX to explore custom workflow options with engineering team for scaling](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=1303.0)
  - [Team to test batch processing of "a couple hundred" vendor pages in shorter timeframe](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=1190.0)
  - [GrowthX transitioning to custom platform in coming weeks to enable better scaling](https://fathom.video/share/6i74Pp75z3yyGa_W3Rd79QsLXcmb46-d?tab=summary&timestamp=1296.0)

action_items.description: Review first 5-10 zero traffic page rewrites; refine brand guidelines based on output
Send list of zero traffic page URLs to Matt via Airtable for review
Provide data wrapper ID for vendor pages to Vivek's team
Add main pages for vendor content in addition to alternatives/versus pages; complete by tomorrow
Review content production workflow for vendor pages; consider optimization strategies
Provide specific target number of vendor pages to be created in next batch
Investigate possibility of API integration with Sanity for direct publishing
